recalls.Description:
Goregent has issued a recall for their infant walkers due to serious safety concerns. These walkers, which are green with an animal print fabric seat, feature a rotating activity tray equipped with toys, lights, and music. They have a round base with six wheels and are designed to be collapsible with three adjustable height settings. The model number 901 and SKU GEBA030AGXP can be found on a yellow label on the base of the walker, with a production date of November 2025.

The recalled walkers do not meet mandatory safety standards, as they can pass through standard doorways and do not stop at the edge of steps, increasing the risk of falls that could lead to severe injury or even death. Consumers are urged to take immediate action regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: Goregent Infant Walkers Recalled Due to Fall Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, please contact Goregent Official Store via email at GoregentInfantWalkersRecall@outlook.com.

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to non-compliance with safety standards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the walkers and request a full refund from Goregent Official Store. Disassemble the walker, remove the seat, mark the tray as 'Recalled,' and send a photo to GoregentInfantWalkersRecall@outlook.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Goregent recalls infant walkers due to fall hazards. Immediate action required for safety and refunds.

recalls.Description:
Bicystar has issued a recall for its children's high chairs due to serious safety hazards. These gray high chairs feature a metal frame, metal legs, a tray, and a cushioned seat cover. The brand name "BICYSTAR" is displayed on the front of the tray, and the model number "TB-HC6900" can be found on a label stitched to the back of the seat cover. The absence of a required crotch restraint increases the risk of falls, while the design allows for a child's head to become trapped, presenting a significant entrapment hazard.

Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled high chairs immediately. To obtain a full refund, contact Bicystar Store and follow the instructions for safe disposal of the product. This includes disassembling the high chair, cutting the restraints and fabric seat cover, marking the backseat with the word "Recall," and sending photographic evidence of the destroyed high chairs to the provided email address.
recalls.Title: Bicystar High Chairs Recalled Due to Fall and Entrapment Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Contact Bicystar Store via email at bicystarhighchair@outlook.com.

recalls.Description:
Sanven Technology has announced a recall of Vevor-branded safety gates due to a serious entrapment hazard. These gates, available in gray, black, and white, feature a metal frame standing approximately 34.2 inches tall with a retractable screen that can extend to various widths: 60 inches (model YJ-3355), 76.8 inches (model YJ-3371), or 116 inches (model YJ-33110). The brand name 'Vevor' is labeled on the gate's corner post, and the model number can be found on the packaging and user manual.

The gates do not comply with the mandatory safety standards for expansion gates and expandable enclosures, allowing a child's torso to fit through the gap between the gate and the floor, which poses a significant risk of injury. Consumers are urged to stop using these gates immediately and reach out to Sanven Technology for a full refund.
recalls.Title: Sanven Technology Issues Recall for Vevor Baby Gates Due to Entrapment Risk and Safety Standard Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Sanven Technology at 855-599-6320 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email recalling@vevor.com, or visit https://www.vevor.com/pages/Vevor-Retractable-Baby-Gates-Recall for more details.

recalls.Description:
The Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy has been recalled due to serious safety concerns. This toy features an off-white disc with either a red or blue ball at its center, surrounded by six colorful silicone pull strings. It also includes three spinning rings and soft buttons on the disc. The packaging displays the model number 688-59. The design of the silicone strings poses a choking risk, as they can become lodged in a child's throat, leading to respiratory distress or even death.

Consumers are urged to stop using the toy immediately and keep it away from children. To ensure safety, contact Longyanguiheng for a full refund. Additionally, consumers should cut and dispose of the silicone strings, mark the toy as "DESTROYED" with a permanent marker, and send a photo of the destroyed toy to yetonamr_recall@163.com.

The pillows are identifiable by the "Greatale" label stitched into the seam and were sold in white with angel wing decorations.

These bath seats feature two detachable arms for restraint, four suction cups for stability, and three toys: a duck, a turtle, and a whale. The model number YD-1958 can be found on a label on the back of the seat. The seats fail to meet safety standards, making them unstable and prone to tipping over during use.
recalls.Title: YCXXKJ Baby Bath Seats Recalled Due to Drowning Hazard; Sold by BenTalk on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact BenTalk at 213-579-2588 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email bentalkhere@outlook.com.

recalls.Description: Uuoeebb Infant Walkers are being recalled due to serious safety concerns, including risks of falls and entrapment. These collapsible walkers feature a round base, eight wheels, and seven adjustable height settings, and were available in gray, black, and pink. The tracking label under the seat indicates "Production Batch: 7654". The design flaws violate mandatory safety standards for infant walkers.
recalls.Title: Uuoeebb Infant Walkers Recalled Due to Fall and Entrapment Risks; Sold on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact BaoD via email at Uuoeebbrecalls@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Uuoeebb Infant Walker in gray
"Production Batch: 7654" on tracking label under the seat

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from December 2024 through September 2025, priced between $60 and $90.

Distributors.Name: Hunan Suihuo E-commerce Co., Ltd., dba BaoD, of China

Hazards.Name: The recalled infant walkers can fit through standard doorways and do not stop at steps, leading to fall hazards. Additionally, the leg openings may allow a child to slip down, potentially causing head entrapment and serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled walkers immediately and reach out to BaoD for a full refund. Disassemble the walker, remove the fabric seat, mark the tray with "Recalled" using a permanent marker, and email a photo of the walker to BaoD at Uuoeebbrecalls@outlook.com. Dispose of the walker following local waste disposal regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Uuoeebb Infant Walkers recalled due to fall and entrapment hazards. Contact BaoD for refunds and disposal instructions.

recalls.Description: Primark is recalling the Little Bear 2-pack Soother Clips, which feature a cloth strap adorned with small bears and a wooden button designed to attach to a child's clothing for pacifier holding. The product code 991139699 can be found on the price tag on the back of the packaging. Safety concerns arise as the wooden button may detach, revealing a sharp screw that poses choking and laceration hazards.
recalls.Title: Primark Recalls Little Bear Soother Clips Due to Choking and Laceration Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Primark at 617-946-3236 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.primark.com/recall. You can also find details by clicking "Recalls" at the bottom of the Primark homepage.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of the wooden buttons on the soother clip detaching, including one report where the screw was found inside an infant's mouth and a second report of cuts to a child's arm.

Retailers.Name: Primark stores in the northeast, Florida, Maryland, and Illinois, sold from June 2025 to August 2025 for approximately $5

Importers.Name: Primark US Corp., of Boston, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Choking and laceration hazards due to the wooden button detaching

Remedies.Name: Stop using the clips and return them to any Primark store for a full refund

recalls.metaDescription: Primark recalls Little Bear Soother Clips due to choking and laceration hazards. Return for a full refund.

recalls.Description: Little Pea Shop is recalling two styles of crib bumpers due to serious safety concerns. The "Waffle Bedding Accessory" bumpers were available in gray, pink, blue, and white, offered in lengths of 23.5 inches, 47 inches, 78.5 inches, and 94.5 inches. The "Plush Crib Bumper" style featured vertical slat covers designed with Velcro in beige and ivory, shaped like bears, rabbits, or stars. These products pose a suffocation risk and are banned under federal law.
recalls.Title: Recall of Little Pea Shop Crib Bumpers Due to Suffocation Hazard; Violates Federal Safety Regulations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, contact Little Pea Shop via email at support@littlepeashop.com or visit https://littlepeashop.com/pages/recall. You can also navigate to their website at https://littlepeashop.com/ and click on "Recall" for more information.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the crib bumpers immediately and contact Little Pea Shop for a full refund. Cut the padding from the bumpers and email a photo of the destroyed items to support@littlepeashop.com.

recalls.Description: Rotinyard has issued a recall for convertible baby high chairs available in beige or pink, featuring a removable seat cushion, detachable tray, and footrest. These high chairs can transform into an infant swing and are designed to be reclinable and collapsible. Look for the tracking label marked 'Product No.: HA-028' on the seatback for identification.
recalls.Title: Convertible Baby High Chairs Recalled for Serious Fall and Entrapment Risks; Sold by Rotinyard on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, email Rotinyard at trailblazeramz@outlook.com.

Hazards.Name: These high chairs do not meet mandatory safety standards due to the absence of an attached restraint system, creating a significant fall risk for infants. Additionally, there is a danger of a child's head becoming trapped between the seat and tray, leading to potential entrapment.

These loungers, which feature a foam sleeping pad and padded bumpers, are rectangular and lack proper safety markings. The Sofoliana model is dark gray with a ribbon tie, while the Glotika model zips into a bag with an adjustable strap and comes in colors like gray-rocket, pink-heart, and white-elephant. Both models have design features that do not comply with safety standards for infant sleep products.
recalls.Title: Recall of Sofoliana and Glotika Baby Loungers Due to Entrapment and Fall Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach out to Bosen US via email at BosenRecall@outlook.com.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Bosen Trading Co., Ltd., dba Bosen US, of China

Hazards.Name: These loungers do not meet mandatory safety standards for infant sleep products, with low sides and wide openings that increase the risk of falls and entrapment, potentially leading to serious injury or death.

The brand name 'Ikuso' is marked on the latch at the top, while the model numbers can be found on labels on the side-post beneath the latch.

recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Ikuso via email at ikuso_door_gate@163.com.

These loungers feature a foam sleeping pad, padded bumpers, and a cloth cover, with a buckle located at one end. They were available in various colors and prints, including blue, brown, brown leaves, and pink flower, with "Macardac" labeled on an external tag on the cover.
recalls.Title: Recall of Macardac Baby Loungers Due to Entrapment and Fall Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Macardac Products via email at macardacrecall@gmail.com.

recalls.Description:
AliExpress has issued a recall for the 4-in-1 Baby Safety Cart Carriage, a convertible stroller that can also be used as a hand-held infant carrier. Available in various colors including pink, blue, green, and black, this product features larger wheels with yellow accents and includes a gray or black infant insert. The recall is prompted by a serious safety concern, as the stroller's restraint system may fail, leading to a risk of severe injury or fatal falls. Consumers are urged to stop using the stroller immediately to ensure their child's safety.

recalls.Description: Sanven Technology has recalled Vevor Baby Swings due to a serious suffocation hazard. The affected swings are available in white/gray and black/gray color combinations, featuring the 'VEVOR' trademark on the base. Each swing is marked with 'Multi-Function Electric Swing' and a model number located on the bottom or back of the base. The model numbers include BB501K for white/gray swings with a white base, BB702A for white/gray swings with a gray base, and BB005 for black/gray swings with a black base.
recalls.Title: Sanven Technology Issues Recall for Vevor Baby Swings Due to Suffocation Risk and Safety Standard Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Sanven Technology at 855-599-6320 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email recalling@vevor.com, or visit https://www.vevor.com/pages/vevor-baby-swing-recall for further details.

recalls.Description:
The recall concerns Creekliybear aftermarket play yard mattresses, specifically designed for 'Pack and Play' models. These mattresses are white with gray stripes and feature labels indicating 'Play Mat' and 'Model: m-sy2'. Their dimensions are approximately 26 inches in width and 38 inches in length. The mattresses do not comply with mandatory crib mattress standards, which raises significant safety concerns. 
The primary hazard associated with these mattresses is that they exceed the allowable thickness and may not fit securely in certain play yards. This can lead to dangerous gaps where infants can become trapped, posing a suffocation risk if their faces are pressed against the sides of the mattress and the play yard walls. 
Consumers are urged to stop using these mattresses immediately and to reach out to Kbabyea for a full refund. To process the refund, consumers must cut the mattresses in half and provide a photo of the cut mattress disposed of in a waste bin.

recalls.Description:
Nezmdi has issued a recall for their electric infant swings due to serious safety concerns, including a suffocation hazard. These swings, designed for infants, feature a pink fabric with a white base and a black control panel, along with plush toys, a pillow, a seat cover, and a mosquito net with a canopy. Each swing measures approximately 25.5 inches in length and width, and 20.8 inches in height, and includes a remote control and USB cable. A leather patch displaying the brand name 'Nezmdi' is sewn onto the restraints, and the model number 'S0008' is labeled beneath the seat.  

The swings were marketed for infant sleep but have an incline angle exceeding the safe limit, violating the Safe Sleep for Babies Act. Additionally, the remote control contains a lithium coin battery that lacks necessary safety warnings, posing further risks to infants.

recalls.Description: Boyro Baby has issued a recall for their convertible high chairs due to serious safety concerns. recalls.Description:
HabiLife Direct is recalling retractable safety mesh gates, identified by model number SG021, due to a significant risk of entrapment that could lead to severe injury or death for children. These gates are available in white, gray, and black, standing approximately 34 inches tall and adjustable up to 55 inches wide, featuring an aluminum frame. recalls.Description:
Gimars Playpens, identified by model numbers GWL-01 through GWL-06, are under recall due to serious safety concerns. These playpens, labeled as "Gimars Playpen with mat," feature a dark gray, black, or light gray fabric with white mesh sides. A reversible mattress with playful designs and a basketball hoop accessory are included. However, the design poses potential entrapment and suffocation hazards for children.

Consumers are urged to stop using these playpens immediately and keep children away from them. The basketball hoop can create a head entrapment risk, and children may become trapped beneath the mattress. This recall is a violation of federal regulations for play yards, necessitating immediate action from consumers.
recalls.Title: Gimars Playpens Recalled for Entrapment and Suffocation Risks; Noncompliance with Federal Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Shenzhen Jingdu Technology via email at Gimars-Aftersales@outlook.com, or visit https://gimarsrecall.com/ or https://gimarsbaby.com and click on "Safety Recalls" for further details.

recalls.Description:
Mamibaby and Cosy Nation have issued a recall for their baby loungers, which feature a cloth cover, foam sleeping pad, and padded bumper. Marketed as "baby loungers" and "co-sleepers," these products come in various designs including Animal, Dinosaur, Giraffe, and more. A tag stating "SLEEPING SET" is sewn inside, while the brand name appears on the exterior of most loungers. Some loungers may lack these tags altogether.

The recall is prompted by serious safety concerns, including the loungers' failure to meet federal safety standards for infant sleep products. The low sides and thick sleeping pad increase the risk of suffocation, while infants may fall out or become trapped. Additionally, the absence of a stand raises the risk of falls when used on elevated surfaces, creating an unsafe environment for babies.
recalls.Title: Recall of Mamibaby and Cosy Nation Baby Loungers Due to Safety Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact MEIXIA Shop via email at recallemail@163.com.

recalls.Description: Jool Baby is recalling its Nova Infant Swings, produced between June 2022 and September 2023. These swings, which are gray and measure approximately 28 inches long, 19 inches wide, and 24 inches high, feature a round aluminum base with music buttons, a metal seat frame, a cloth seat with restraints and headrest, and a canopy adorned with hanging toys shaped like a yellow moon, blue cloud, and pink star. The swings can be powered by AAA batteries or an AC adapter and come with an electronic remote control. The brand name 'Jool Baby' is located on the swing's base, while 'NOVA' can be found on the seat label. The manufacturing date is indicated on the sewn-in warning label on the back of the swing's seat.
recalls.Title: Jool Baby Recalls Nova Infant Swings Due to Suffocation Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Jool Baby toll-free at 888-788-5665 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Thursday, and from 10 a.m. to 1 p.m. ET on Friday. They can also email recall@JoolBaby.com or visit www.JoolBaby.com/recall for further information.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart stores and online at www.JoolBaby.com, www.amazon.com, www.babylist.com, www.target.com, www.walmart.com, and other retailers from November 2022 to November 2023, priced around $150.

Importers.Name: Jool Products LLC, dba Jool Baby, of Lakewood, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to improper design for infant sleep, exceeding the safe incline angle, and failure to meet warning requirements.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the swings for sleep and contact Jool Baby for a free repair kit, which includes shipping. A photo of the warning label must be uploaded to the online registration page at www.JoolBaby.com/recall. After registration, a repair kit with a new seat featuring updated warnings, plush toys, and a new remote control will be sent.

recalls.metaDescription: Jool Baby recalls Nova Infant Swings due to suffocation hazards. Learn how to get a free repair kit and ensure your baby's safety.

recalls.Description: The SINGES 3-in-1 Baby High Chairs and Booster Seats have been recalled due to safety violations that pose a fall risk to children. These high chairs feature a light blue cushioned seat, detachable legs, and an adjustable tray that allows conversion into a booster seat. They were available in blue and red colors. The product does not meet federal safety regulations for high chairs and booster seats, with multiple safety failures identified, including issues with the restraint system and stability.

recalls.Description: The Spring Spirit and Biloban pack and play mattresses have been recalled due to safety violations and a potential suffocation hazard for infants. The Spring Spirit mattress features a trifold design with a bamboo pattern and was manufactured between August 2022 and July 2023. It includes identifiable tags with the batch number 230315US. The Biloban mattress is white with gray stars and was also produced from August 2022 to July 2023, marked with batch number 2304US. Both mattresses lack necessary safety warnings and labels, violating federal crib mattress regulations.

recalls.Description: Brandline is recalling its HEAO 4-in-1 high chairs due to serious safety concerns including risks of suffocation, entrapment, and laceration. These high chairs, available in various colors such as black, blue, gold, green, gray, pink, and red, feature adjustable height, multiple recline positions, a footrest, a detachable tray, seat cushion, and four wheels with locks. The brand name "HEAO" can be found on the head support of the seat cushion and on the front of the tray. A label with the manufacturer’s information is stitched on the back of the seat cushion.
recalls.Title: Brandline Recalls HEAO 4-in-1 High Chairs Due to Suffocation, Entrapment, and Laceration Risks

recalls.Description:
Vibe Bear is recalling its portable playyard mattresses manufactured on or after August 15, 2022, due to serious safety concerns. These mattresses are characterized by a white color with a diagonal quilted design and feature illustrations of paintbrushes, paints, and nature. They measure approximately 38 inches in length, 26 inches in width, and 3 inches in height, with a dark blue tag displaying a cartoon teddy bear. The model number YX-B17 and the manufacturing date can be found on the tag located at the edge of the mattress. The design includes a crease down the center for easy folding.

The recall is prompted by violations of federal safety regulations for crib mattresses, specifically regarding firmness and thickness standards, and the absence of necessary warnings and labels. This poses a suffocation risk to infants, making it imperative for consumers to take immediate action regarding the recalled products.
recalls.Title: Vibe Bear Playyard Mattresses Recalled Due to Infant Suffocation Risks
